cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this question out. The passage is about how supermarkets use various marketing tactics to influence customers. There's a part about three jars of low-fat milk. The question is asking which of the options is correct regarding these jars. First, I need to recall what the passage said. Let me re-read the relevant section. It says: "One store has three different jars of low fat milk. One says '1 percent fat' on the jar. The second says '99 percent fat free'. The third says 'LOW FAT' in big letters and '1%' in small letters. As you can see, all the milk has the same amount of fat. The milk is all the same. However, in this store the three jars of milk cost three different amounts of money. Maybe the customers will buy the milk that costs the most." So the key point here is that all three jars have the same amount of fat. The labels are different, but the actual fat content is the same. Therefore, the answer should reflect that they all have the same amount of fat. Looking at the options: (A) one has more fat than the other two – This contradicts the passage. The passage says they all have the same. (B) they all cost the same amount of money – No, the passage says
